summary.what: Carry the commit → log → push order in the compact prime Rules block
summary.why: The compact prime is the default session-start injection (timbers prime --hook); it omitted the push-ordering rule, so #283's fix for the push-before-log race was invisible on the path agents actually see — the fix lived only in prime --full and MCP
summary.how: Added one Rules bullet with a brief motivation; added a compact-prime test assertion and mutation-checked it

notes: Surfaced by an osprey-strike workaround-sweep: a downstream agent nearly deleted its repo-local order clause assuming prime carried it fleet-wide. It does — but only in --full/MCP, not the compact hook output. Kept it a distinct bullet (not folded into the log line) because ordering is a separate rule from 'log after commit' and deserves its own scannable line, mirroring how protocol.go separates them.
